fields.description:
# WS6 — Gerrit LLM-Review voter + dogfood

## Why
The dogfood success criterion: rebar's own changes are reviewed and gated end-to-end through Gerrit's `LLM-Review` label. This is the only workstream gated on external infra (the AWS Gerrit + rebar MCP provisioning epic d251-05e1-eb55-43b2, OPEN).

## What
- On `patchset-created`, run the four-pass code-review gate over the change and vote `LLM-Review` via Gerrit REST; the deterministic block/advisory outcome maps to the label.
- Prove end-to-end: at least one real rebar change reviewed + gated on the AWS/Gerrit environment.

## Scope
- The Gerrit voter integration (event wiring + REST vote) in the rebar MCP server.
- OUT OF SCOPE: the capability itself (WS1-WS5); the CI "Verified" second vote (epic 1fa8).

## Dependencies
- BLOCKED ON d251-05e1-eb55-43b2 (AWS Gerrit + rebar MCP provisioning). The epic-level b744<-d251 blocker already enforces this; this story cannot complete until d251 lands.

## Specifics pinned (plan-review remediation 2026-06)
- **Provisioned by `d251` (this story CONSUMES them):** the `LLM-Review` label definition, the submit requirement (`label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved`), the `copyCondition` (vote-carry across trivial rebases — which also governs vote-reset on a new patchset), and the rebar-MCP→Gerrit auth identity (service account / token).
- **Vote mapping:** deterministic BLOCK → `LLM-Review -1` (veto; blocks submit); PASS/advisory → `LLM-Review +1/MAX` (satisfies d251's `=MAX` submit requirement).
- **Two separable deliverables:** (a) the voter integration (event wiring + REST vote) is codeable + testable against a STUB Gerrit now; (b) the dogfood end-state is the only part gated on `d251`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On patchset-created, the gate reviews the change, runs the four passes, and votes `LLM-Review` via REST; block/advisory maps to the label value.
- [ ] Dogfood proof: at least one real rebar change is reviewed + gated end-to-end on the AWS/Gerrit environment (record the change URL + the resulting vote on this ticket).

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- Staged rollout: the voter activates behind a per-project/staged opt-in (config gate) before fleet-wide — a BLOCK from an under-tested voter does not gate ALL committers on day one.
- Fail-closed BLOCK surfaced DISTINCTLY: a -1 caused by detector-unavailability (WS5 coverage-gap) is labeled distinctly in the Gerrit robot comment so an infra-failure -1 is distinguishable from a real-finding -1.
- Shares d251's reconciled adapter seam `code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref) → {decision: PASS|BLOCK}` (see d251 Round-11): WS6 reimplements that SAME signature over `produce_code_review_verdict`; the d251 receiver does not change on swap.

Added ACs:
- [ ] Voter activation is staged (per-project/config opt-in) before fleet-wide; documented
- [ ] A fail-closed/coverage-gap -1 is surfaced distinctly from a real-finding -1 in the Gerrit vote/robot comment
- [ ] WS6 implements the `code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref)→{decision}` adapter (the d251-S4b seam) over `produce_code_review_verdict`; receiver unchanged on swap
